evals.prompt:
Read the existing Drupal module at /tmp/phase21-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/ to understand its current code. This module has Project and Task content entities (entity type IDs: 'project' and 'task') with CRUD operations, access control handlers with granular permissions (view/edit own/edit any/delete own/delete any for both entity types), a settings form at /admin/config/group_ai_pm/settings, a dashboard controller at /admin/content/project-dashboard that uses a #theme render array with a Twig template and attached CSS library, a ProjectStatusBlock block plugin, Views integration with a computed ProjectTaskCount views field, hook_cron for overdue task detection with Lock API, a QueueWorker for processing overdue notifications, REST API endpoints (GET /api/kanban/project/{project} for board data, PATCH /api/kanban/task/{task}/status for status update, PATCH /api/kanban/task/{task} for field update, POST /api/kanban/project/{project}/task for quick create, DELETE /api/kanban/task/{task} for delete, GET /api/kanban/project/{project}/summary for project summary), a Vue.js Kanban board page at /admin/content/project/{project}/board with drag-and-drop task management, composables (toast, filters, contextMenu, displayOptions), TaskDetailPanel, ContextMenu, FilterBar, DisplayOptions, AssigneeAvatar, ToastContainer components, enhanced TaskCard with inline edit and due dates, and a TaskStatusForm with AJAX status dropdowns at /admin/content/task/overview.

The module already has 6 existing test files that you must NOT modify or duplicate:
- tests/src/Kernel/EntityCrudTest.php (entity CRUD)
- tests/src/Kernel/AccessControlTest.php (permission-based access)
- tests/src/Kernel/CronQueueTest.php (cron + queue)
- tests/src/Functional/SettingsFormTest.php (settings form)
- tests/src/Functional/DashboardTest.php (dashboard page)
- tests/src/Functional/EntityFormIntegrationTest.php (entity forms)

Write NEW PHPUnit tests for the v4.0 features added in Phases 18-20 (REST API and board page). Create exactly 2 new test classes:

1. **Kernel Test for REST API (tests/src/Kernel/RestApiTest.php)**: Test the custom REST API endpoints using Symfony's HttpKernelInterface to make programmatic HTTP requests. REST endpoints CAN be tested in Kernel tests via $this->container->get('http_kernel')->handle(Request::create(...)). Test:
   a. GET /api/kanban/project/{project_id}?_format=json returns 200 with JSON body containing columns with task data grouped by status
   b. GET /api/kanban/project/{project_id}/summary?_format=json returns 200 with project summary data
   c. Access denied (403) for a user without 'access group_ai_pm dashboard' permission on the kanban endpoint

2. **Functional Test for Board Page (tests/src/Functional/BoardPageTest.php)**: Test the Kanban board page rendering using BrowserTestBase. Test:
   a. Board page at /admin/content/project/{project_id}/board renders with a #kanban-app mount point element
   b. Board page includes drupalSettings in the page output
   c. The 'Board' local task tab exists on the project canonical page
   d. Anonymous user gets 403 on the board page

Create all test files in /tmp/phase21-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/tests/
After creating all files, run: cd /tmp/phase21-VARIANT && ddev drush cr 2>/dev/null; true
Do NOT ask questions -- just create the code.
evals.expected_output: Two new test files: a Kernel RestApiTest using HttpKernelInterface for REST endpoint testing, and a Functional BoardPageTest using BrowserTestBase for board page rendering, with correct setUp patterns, test discovery annotations, base class selection, and module dependencies.
expectations: The REST API test class extends KernelTestBase (NOT BrowserTestBase) — the testing skill's decision tree says: 'Needs database, services, config, or entities but NOT browser interaction? YES -> Kernel test'. REST endpoints return JSON, not HTML pages, so KernelTestBase with HttpKernelInterface is the correct and fastest approach. Using BrowserTestBase for JSON endpoint testing is 10-100x slower and violates the LOWEST sufficient level principle.

The Board page test class extends BrowserTestBase (NOT KernelTestBase) — the testing skill's decision tree says: 'Needs page navigation, form submission, HTTP responses? YES -> Functional test'. The board page test needs to verify HTML rendering (#kanban-app mount point, drupalSettings output, local task tabs), which requires browser simulation that KernelTestBase cannot provide.

Every test class has a @group annotation set to 'group_ai_pm' in the class-level PHPDoc comment (the drupal-testing skill explicitly warns: 'Without @group, the test will not be discovered by Drupal test runner or when running phpunit --group module_name' — this is required for test discovery, not optional documentation)

The Kernel test class declares a protected static $modules array that includes 'group_ai_pm' AND 'system' AND 'user' at minimum (the testing skill warns: 'Missing modules cause cryptic Service not found or Table not found errors. Include ALL dependency modules your code needs' — 'system' provides core services like routing, 'user' is needed for entity owner references and permission checking)

The Kernel test $modules array includes the 'datetime' module (the Task entity has a due_date field of type 'datetime' — the testing skill explicitly states: 'datetime fields need the datetime module' in the WRONG/RIGHT callout about field module dependencies. Without it, entity creation fails with 'Unknown field type datetime')

The Kernel test $modules array includes the 'text' module (entities use text fields like description — the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'text_long fields need the text module'. Without it, text field types are not registered and entity save fails)

The Kernel test $modules array includes the 'options' module (the Task entity uses list_string fields for status and priority — the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'list_string fields need the options module'. Without it, the list_string field type is not available)

The Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('project') to create the Project entity database tables (the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'Unlike full Drupal installs, Kernel tests do NOT run hook_schema() automatically. Custom tables will not exist, causing Table not found errors' — entity tables must be explicitly installed in Kernel tests)

The Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('task') to create the Task entity database tables (same rationale as project — both entity types are needed for REST API tests that create projects with tasks)

The Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('user') to install the user entity schema (both Project and Task entities have entity_reference fields pointing to User entities via 'uid' — saving entities with user references without user schema installed causes foreign key or storage failures)

The Kernel test setUp() calls $this->installConfig(['group_ai_pm']) to install the module's default configuration (REST API controllers may read module config, and entity field definitions depend on installed config — the testing skill shows installConfig as part of the standard Kernel setUp pattern)

Every setUp() method in both test classes calls parent::setUp() as the FIRST statement before any other setup code (the testing skill's WRONG/RIGHT callout: 'Forgetting to call parent::setUp() or calling it after your setup code... the parent setUp() bootstraps the test environment. Without it, tests fail with cryptic errors')

The BrowserTestBase test class declares protected $defaultTheme = 'stark' (the testing skill's CRITICAL NEVER callout: 'Do NOT use $defaultTheme = starterkit_theme or any starter/generated theme. Use ONLY stark or claro.' — using starterkit_theme causes test failures because it is not installed in test environments)

Test files are placed in the correct directory structure: the Kernel test in tests/src/Kernel/ and the Functional test in tests/src/Functional/ (NOT tests/Kernel/, src/Tests/, or any other non-standard path — the testing skill shows the exact required directory layout: 'tests/src/Kernel/ -> extends KernelTestBase, tests/src/Functional/ -> extends BrowserTestBase')

Test class namespaces follow the pattern Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Kernel\ClassName for the Kernel test and Drupal\Tests\group_ai_pm\Functional\ClassName for the Functional test, matching the directory structure (the testing skill shows: 'tests/src/Kernel/ImporterTest.php -> Drupal\Tests\my_module\Kernel\ImporterTest' — wrong namespace means the class cannot be autoloaded and PHPUnit will not discover it)
